content.body: For today's continuation discussion on TypedArray `concat` ... I've been able to update the problem statement in a way that addresses dminor's concerns but there's still enough of an open question around iterable support that I'm going to defer asking for Stage 2 and instead just see if we can settle some of the questions around iterable support as documented in this comment: https://github.com/tc39/proposal-typedarray-concat/issues/8#issuecomment-4045546879 ... mentioning here now so folks interested in the discussion might have some time to gather thoughts

content.body: @jasnell:matrix.org I'm really worried about `lastIndexOfSequence`. That sounds like it gives you the index at the end of the sequence. I think it should be `indexOfLastSequence` or something.
content.formatted_body: @James M Snell I'm really worried about lastIndexOfSequence. That sounds like it gives you the index at the end of the sequence. I think it should be indexOfLastSequence or something.

content.body: Fair
I do not have the same intuition about those names fwiw
I'm terrible at naming so I'm happy to go with whatever suggestion seems the best to y'all so that's a change I can make :-)
"last index of sequence" sounds like it gives the last index where the sequence is
which... is what it does
The meeting is starting in 30 seconds!!!
seems ambiguous at best
yeah the last index where the first occurrence of the sequence is
content.formatted_body: yeah the last index where the first occurrence of the sequence is

content.body: if we didn't already have `lastIndexOf` I could maybe imagine having that intuition, but we do
content.formatted_body: if we didn't already have lastIndexOf I could maybe imagine having that intuition, but we do

content.body:
alternative idea: drop the second operation entirely and allow a native (search from the end) position argument on...

```
haystack.indexOfSequence(needle, -10);  // search forward from 10 elements from the end, etc
```
content.formatted_body:
alternative idea: drop the second operation entirely and allow a native (search from the end) position argument on...

haystack.indexOfSequence(needle, -10);  // search forward from 10 elements from the end, etc

content.body: `indexOf` already supports a negative index and it's very useful
content.formatted_body: indexOf already supports a negative index and it's very useful

content.body: this is not a substitute for `lastIndexOf`
content.formatted_body: this is not a substitute for lastIndexOf

content.body: also fair.... hmm
from prior art, it's worth noting that in Node.js `Buffer`, they overload `indexOf`/`lastIndexOf` for this.. e.g. `Buffer.from('hellohello').lastIndexOf('hello') === 5`
content.formatted_body: from prior art, it's worth noting that in Node.js Buffer, they overload indexOf/lastIndexOf for this.. e.g. Buffer.from('hellohello').lastIndexOf('hello') === 5

content.body: not suggesting we do the same but `lastIndexOfSequence` would follow that existing pattern
content.formatted_body: not suggesting we do the same but lastIndexOfSequence would follow that existing pattern

content.body: We just need some parentheses -- `haystack["last(IndexOfSequence)"](needle)`
haystack.findTheLastOccurrenceOfThisSequence(needle) ;-)
@sffc:mozilla.org Correct, the entirety of the proposal aside from syntax can be implemented in a polyfill.
content.formatted_body: @sffc Correct, the entirety of the proposal aside from syntax can be implemented in a polyfill.

Curious what others beyond Michael Ficarra and bakkot feel is the better / less ambiguous / less confusion choice

content.body: while folks are considering that... for TypedArray concat naming... I'm thinking of going with `%TypeArray%.fromList([...])`, `ArrayBuffer.fromList([...])`, and `SharedArrayBuffer.fromList([...])` since `concat` appears to not be workable
content.formatted_body: while folks are considering that... for TypedArray concat naming... I'm thinking of going with %TypeArray%.fromList([...]), ArrayBuffer.fromList([...]), and SharedArrayBuffer.fromList([...]) since concat appears to not be workable

content.body: yeah that's the same reasoning I've heard for why people don't like spies, and I came to a similar conclusion 😄
objectively (a) is ambiguous and (b) is not, although the degree to which the average developer is vulnerable to the ambiguity in (a) is an empirical question
maybe `locateSequence` and `locateLastSequence` avoids the issue?
